m,\n                                const float *__restrict__ unknown,\n                                const float *__restrict__ known,\n                                float *__restrict__ dist2,\n                                int *__restrict__ idx) {\n  int batch_index = blockIdx.x;\n  unknown += batch_index * n * 3;\n  known += batch_index * m * 3;\n  dist2 += batch_index * n * 3;\n  idx += batch_index * n * 3;\n\n  int index = threadIdx.x;\n  int stride = blockDim.x;\n  for (int j = index; j < n; j += stride) {\n    float ux = unknown[j * 3 + 0];\n    float uy = unknown[j * 3 + 1];\n    float uz = unknown[j * 3 + 2];\n\n    double best1 = 1e40, best2 = 1e40, best3 = 1e40;\n    int besti1 = 0, besti2 = 0, besti3 = 0;\n    for (int k = 0; k < m; ++k) {\n      float x = known[k * 3 + 0];\n      float y = known[k * 3 + 1];\n      float z = known[k * 3 + 2];\n      float d = (ux - x) * (ux - x) + (uy - y) * (uy - y) + (uz - z) * (uz - z);\n      if (d < best1) {\n        best3 = best2;\n        besti3 = besti2;\n        best2 = best1;\n        besti2 = besti1;\n        best1 = d;\n        besti1 = k;\n      } else if (d < best2) {\n        best3 = best2;\n        besti3 = besti2;\n        best2 = d;\n        besti2 = k;\n      } else if (d < best3) {\n        best3 = d;\n        besti3 = k;\n      }\n    }\n    dist2[j * 3 + 0] = best1;\n    dist2[j * 3 + 1] = best2;\n    dist2[j * 3 + 2] = best3;\n\n    idx[j * 3 + 0] = besti1;\n    idx[j * 3 + 1] = besti2;\n    idx[j * 3 + 2] = besti3;\n  }\n}\n\nvoid three_nn_kernel_wrapper(int b, int n, int m, const float         const int *__restrict__ idx,\n                                         const float *__restrict__ weight,\n                                         float *__restrict__ out) {\n  int batch_index points[l * m + i2] * w2 +\n             points[l * m + i3] * w3;\n  }\n}\n\nvoid three_interpolate_kernel_wrapper(int b, int c, int m, int n,\n                                      const float grad_out[i] * w3);\n  }\n}\n\nvoid three_interpolate_grad_kernel_wrapper(int b, int c, int n, int m,\n                                           const float *grad_out,\n type: (QueryAndGroup, float, int, bool) -> None\n        super(QueryAndGroup, self).__init__()\n        self.radius, self.nsample, self.use_xyz = radius, nsample, use_xyz\n\n    def forward(self, xyz, new_xyz, features=None):\n        # type: (QueryAndGroup, torch.Tensor. torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[Torch.Tensor]\n        r\"\"\"\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        xyz : torch.Tensor\n            xyz coordinates of the features (B, N, 3)\n        new_xyz : torch.Tensor\n            centriods (B, npoint, 3)\n        features : torch.Tensor\n            Descriptors of the features (B, C, N)\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        new_features : torch.Tensor\n            (B, 3 + C, npoint, nsample) tensor\n        \"\"\"\n\n        idx = ball_query(self.radius, self.nsample, xyz, new_xyz)\n        xyz_trans = xyz.transpose(1, 2).contiguous()\n        grouped_xyz = grouping_operation(xyz_trans, idx)  # (B, 3, npoint, nsample)\n        grouped_xyz -= new_xyz.transpose(1, 2).unsqueeze(-1)\n\n        if features is not None:\n            grouped_features = grouping_operation(features, idx)\n            if self.use_xyz:\n                new_features = torch.cat(\n                    [grouped_xyz, grouped_features], dim=1\n                )  # (B, C + 3, npoint, nsample)\n            else:\n                new_features = grouped_features\n        else:\n            assert (\n                self.use_xyz\n            ), \"Cannot have not features and not use xyz as a feature!\"\n            new_features = grouped_xyz\n\n        return new_features\n\n\nclass GroupAll(nn.Module):\n    r\"\"\"\n    Groups all features\n\n    Parameters\n    ---------\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, use_xyz=True):\n        # That interface is intended for runtime\ndispatch and should be used from files that are included in both CPU-only and\nCUDA builds.\n\nCUDAContext, on the other hand, should be preferred by files only included in\nCUDA builds. It is intended to expose CUDA functionality in a consistent\nmanner.\n\nThis means there is some overlap between the CUDAContext and CUDAHooks, but\nthe choice of which to use is simple: use CUDAContext when in a CUDA-only file,\nuse CUDAHooks otherwise.\n\nNote that CUDAContext simply defines an interface with no associated class.\nIt is expected that the modules whose functions compose this interface will\nmanage their own state. If ROCM_PATH is set, assume intention is to compile with\n# ROCm support and error out if the ROCM_PATH does not exist.\n# Else ROCM_PATH does not exist, assume a default of /opt/rocm\n# In the latter case, if /opt/rocm does not exist emit status\n# message and return.\nif(DEFINED ENV{ROCM_PATH})\n  file(TO_CMAKE_PATH \"$ENV{ROCM_PATH}\" ROCM_PATH)\n  if(NOT EXISTS ${ROCM_PATH})\n    message(FATAL_ERROR\n      \"ROCM_PATH environment variable is set to ${ROCM_PATH} but does not exist.\\n\"\n      \"Set a valid ROCM_PATH or unset ROCM_PATH environment variable to fix.\")\n  endif()\nelse()\n  if(UNIX)\n    set(ROCM_PATH /opt/rocm)\n  else() # Win32\n    set(ROCM_PATH C:/opt/rocm)\n  endif()\n  if(NOT EXISTS ${ROCM_PATH})\n    message(STATUS\n        \"ROCM_PATH Check if HIP_VERSION contains a dash (e.g., \"7.1.25421-32f9fa6ca5\")\n    # and strip everything after it to get clean numeric version\n    string(FIND \"${HIP_VERSION}\" \"-\" DASH_POS)\n    if(NOT DASH_POS EQUAL -1)\n      string(SUBSTRING \"${HIP_VERSION}\" 0 ${DASH_POS} HIP_VERSION_CLEAN)\n      set(HIP_VERSION \"${HIP_VERSION_CLEAN}\")\n  endif()\n  message(\"HIP version: ${HIP_VERSION}\")\nendif()\n\n# The rocm-core package was only introduced in ROCm 6.4, so we make it optional.\n  find_package(rocm-core CONFIG)\n\n  # Some old consumer HIP SDKs do not distribute rocm_version.h, so we allow\n  # falling back to the hip version, which everyone should have.\n  # rocm_version.h lives in the rocm-core package and hip_version.h lives in the\n  # hip (lower-case) package. Both are probed above and will be in\n  # ROCM_INCLUDE_DIRS if available.\n  find_file(ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH\n    NAMES rocm-core/rocm_version.h hip/hip_version.h\n    NO_DEFAULT_PATH\n    PATHS ${ROCM_INCLUDE_DIRS}\n  )\n  if(ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH MATCHES \"rocm-core/rocm_version.h$\")\n    set(ROCM_LIB_NAME \"ROCM\")\n  else()\n    set(ROCM_LIB_NAME \"HIP\")\n  endif()\n\n  if(NOT ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH)\n    message(FATAL_ERROR \"Could not find hip/hip_version.h or rocm-core/rocm_version.h in ${ROCM_INCLUDE_DIRS}\")\n  endif()\n  get_filename_component(ROCM_HEADER_NAME ${ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH} NAME)\n\n  if(EXISTS ${ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH})\n    set(ROCM_HEADER_FILE ${ROCM_VERSION_HEADER_PATH})\n  else()\n    message(FATAL_ERROR \"********************* ${ROCM_HEADER_NAME} could not be found ******************\\n\")\n  endif()\n\n  # Read the ROCM headerfile into a variable\n  message(STATUS \"Reading ROCM version from: ${ROCM_HEADER_FILE}\")\n  message(STATUS \"Content: ${ROCM_HEADER_CONTENT}\")\n  file(READ \"${ROCM_HEADER_FILE}\" ROCM_HEADER_CONTENT)\n\n  # Below we use a RegEx to find ROCM version numbers.\n  # Note that CMake does not support \\s for blank space. That is\n  # why in the regular expressions below we have a blank space in\n  # the square brackets.\n  # There are three steps:\n  # 1. Match regular expression\n  # 2. Strip the non-numerical part of the string\n  # 3. Strip leading and trailing spaces\n\n  string(REGEX MATCH \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_MAJOR[ ]+[0-9]+\" TEMP1 ${ROCM_HEADER_CONTENT})\n  string(REPLACE \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_MAJOR\" \"\" TEMP2 ${TEMP1})\n  string(STRIP ${TEMP2} ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MAJOR)\n  string(REGEX MATCH \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_MINOR[ ]+[0-9]+\" TEMP1 ${ROCM_HEADER_CONTENT})\n  string(REPLACE \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_MINOR\" \"\" TEMP2 ${TEMP1})\n  string(STRIP ${TEMP2} ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MINOR)\n  string(REGEX MATCH \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_PATCH[ ]+[0-9]+\" TEMP1 ${ROCM_HEADER_CONTENT})\n  string(REPLACE \"${ROCM_LIB_NAME}_VERSION_PATCH\" \"\" TEMP2 ${TEMP1})\n  string(STRIP ${TEMP2} ROCM_VERSION_DEV_PATCH)\n\n  # Create ROCM_VERSION_DEV_INT which is later used as a preprocessor macros\n  set(ROCM_VERSION_DEV \"${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MAJOR}.${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MINOR}.${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_PATCH}\")\n  math(EXPR ROCM_VERSION_DEV_INT \"(${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MAJOR}*10000) + (${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MINOR}*100) + ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_PATCH}\")\n\n  message(\"\\n***** ROCm version from ${ROCM_HEADER_NAME} ****\\n\")\n  message(\"ROCM_VERSION_DEV: ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV}\")\n  message(\"ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MAJOR: ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MAJOR}\")\n  message(\"ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MINOR: ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_MINOR}\")\n  message(\"ROCM_VERSION_DEV_PATCH: ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_PATCH}\")\n  message(\"ROCM_VERSION_DEV_INT:   ${ROCM_VERSION_DEV_INT}\")\n\n  math(EXPR TORCH_HIP_VERSION \"(${HIP_VERSION_MAJOR} * 100) + ${HIP_VERSION_MINOR}\")\n  message(\"HIP_VERSION_MAJOR: ${HIP_VERSION_MAJOR}\")\n  message(\"HIP_VERSION_MINOR: ${HIP_VERSION_MINOR}\")\n  message(\"TORCH_HIP_VERSION: ${TORCH_HIP_VERSION}\")\n\n  # Find ROCM components using Config mode\n  # These components will be searced for recursively in ${ROCM_PATH}\n  message(\"\\n***** Library versions from cmake find_package *****\\n\")\n  find_package_and_print_version(amd_comgr REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(rocrand REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hiprand REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(rocblas REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipblas REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(miopen REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipfft REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipsparse REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(rocprim REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipcub REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(rocthrust REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipsolver REQUIRED)\n  find_package_and_print_version(rocsolver REQUIRED)\n  # Optional components.\n  find_package_and_print_version(hipsparselt)  # Will be required when ready.\n\n  list(REMOVE_DUPLICATES ROCM_INCLUDE_DIRS)\n\n  if(UNIX)\n    # roctx is part of roctracer\n    find_library(ROCM_ROCTX_LIB roctx64 HINTS ${ROCM_PATH}/lib)\n\n    set(PROJECT_RANDOM_BINARY_DIR \"${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR}\")\n\n    if(ROCM_VERSION_DEV VERSION_GREATER_EQUAL \"5.7.0\")\n      # CUDAStreams\n15:  * are backed by cuStreams, but they use several pools to minimize the costs\n16:  * associated with creating, retaining, and destroying cuStreams.\n17:  *\n18:  * There are three pools per device, and a device's pools are lazily created.\n19:  *\n20:  * The first pool contains only the default stream. When the default stream\n21:  * is requested it's returned.\n22:  *\n23:  * The second pool is the \"low priority\" or \"default priority\" streams. In\n24:  * HIP builds there is no distinction between streams in this pool and streams\n25:  * in the third pool (below). There are 32 of these streams per device, and\n26:  * when a stream is requested one of these streams is returned round-robin.\n27:  * That is, the first stream requested is at index 0, the second at index 1...\n28:  * to index 31, then index 0 again.\n29:  *\n30:  * This means that if 33 low priority streams are requested, the first and\n31:  * last streams requested are actually the same stream (under the covers)\n32:  * and kernels enqueued on them cannot run concurrently.\n33:  *\n34:  * The third pool is the \"high priority\" streams. The third pool acts like\n35:  * the second pool except the streams are created with a higher priority.\n36:  *\n37:  * These pools suggest that stream users should prefer many short-lived streams,\n38:  * as the cost of acquiring and releasing streams is effectively zero. If\n39:  * many longer-lived streams are required in performance critical scenarios\n40:  * then the functionality here may need to be extended to allow, for example,\n41:  * \"reserving\" a subset of the pool so that other streams do not accidentally\n42:  * overlap the performance critical streams.\n43:  *\n44:  * Note: although the notion of \"current stream for device\" is thread local\n45:  * (every OS thread has a separate current stream, as one might expect),\n46:  * the stream pool is global across all threads; stream 0 is always stream 0\n47:  * no matter which thread you use it on.  Multiple threads can synchronize\n48:  * on the same stream.  Although the CUDA documentation is not very clear\n49:  * on the matter, streams are thread safe; e.g., it is safe to enqueue\n50:  * a kernel on the same stream from two different threads.\n51:  */\n52: \n53: namespace c10::cuda {\n54: \n55: static constexpr int max_compile_time_stream_priorities = 4;\n56: \n57: // Value object representing a CUDA stream.  This is just a wrapper\n58: // around c10::Stream, but it comes with a little extra CUDA-specific\n59: // functionality (conversion to cudaStream_t), and a guarantee that\n60: // the wrapped c10::Stream really is a CUDA stream.\n61: class C10_CUDA_API CUDAStream {\n62:  public:\n63:   enum Unchecked { UNCHECKED };\n64: \n65:   /// Construct a CUDAStream from a Stream.  This construction is checked,\n66:   /// and will raise an error if the Stream is not, in fact, a CUDA stream.\n67:   explicit CUDAStream(Stream stream) : stream_(stream) {\n68:     TORCH_CHECK(stream_.device_type() == DeviceType::CUDA);\n69:   }\n70: \n71:   /// Construct a CUDAStream from a Stream with no error checking.\n72:   /// This constructor uses the \"named\" constructor idiom, and can\n73:   /// be invoked as: CUDAStream(CUDAStream::UNCHECKED, stream)\n74:   explicit CUDAStream(Unchecked /*unused*/, Stream stream) : stream_(stream) {}\n75: \n76:   bool operator==(const CUDAStream& other) const noexcept {\n77:     return unwrap() == other.unwrap();\n78:   }\n79: \n80:   bool operator!=(const CUDAStream& other) const noexcept {\n81:     return unwrap() != other.unwrap();\n82:   }\n83: \n84:   /// Implicit conversion to cudaStream_t.\n85:   operator cudaStream_t() const {\n86:     return stream();\n87:   }\n88: \n89:   /// Implicit conversion to Stream (a.k.a., forget that the stream is a\n90:   /// CUDA stream).\n91:   operator Stream() const {\n92:     return unwrap();\n93:   }\n94: \n95:   /// Used to avoid baking in device type explicitly to Python-side API.\n96:   DeviceType device_type() const {\n97:     return DeviceType::CUDA;\n98:   }\n99: \n100:   /// Get the CUDA device index that this stream is associated with.\n\n(Showing lines 1-100 of 273. CUDAStreams\n * are backed by cuStreams, but they use several pools to minimize the costs\n * associated with creating, retaining, and destroying cuStreams.\n *\n * There are three pools per device, and a device's pools are lazily created.\n *\n * The first pool contains only the default stream. When the default stream","truncated":true,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/torch/include/c10/cuda/CUDAStream.h","text":"#if !defined(TORCH_STABLE_ONLY) && !defined(TORCH_TARGET_VERSION)\n#pragma once\n\n#include <cuda_runtime_api.h>\n\n#include <c10/core/DeviceGuard.h>\n#include <c10/core/Stream.h>\n#include <c10/cuda/CUDAFunctions.h>\n#include <c10/util/Exception.h>\n\n/*\n * Stream pool note.\n *\n * A CUDAStream is an abstraction of an actual cuStream on the GPU. CUDAStreams\n * are backed by cuStreams, but they use several pools to minimize the costs\n * associated with creating, retaining, and destroying cuStreams.\n *\n * There are three pools per device, and a device's pools are lazily created.\n *\n * The first pool contains only the default stream. When the default stream\n * is requested it's returned.\n *\n * The second pool is the \"low priority\" or \"default priority\" streams. In\n * HIP builds there is no distinction between streams in this pool and streams\n * in the third pool (below). There are 32 of these streams per device, and\n * when a stream is requested one of these streams is returned round-robin.\n * That is, the first stream requested is at index 0, the second at index 1...\n * to index 31, then index 0 again.\n *\n * This means that if 33 low priority streams are requested, the first and\n * last streams requested are actually the same stream (under the covers)\n * and kernels enqueued on them cannot run concurrently.\n *\n * The third pool is the \"high priority\" streams. The third pool acts like\n * the second pool except the streams are created with a higher priority.\n *\n * These pools suggest that stream users should prefer many short-lived streams,\n * as the cost of acquiring and releasing streams is effectively zero. If\n * many longer-lived streams are required in performance critical scenarios\n * then the functionality here may need to be extended to allow, for example,\n * \"reserving\" a subset of the pool so that other streams do not accidentally\n * overlap the performance critical streams.\n *\n * Note: although the notion of \"current stream for device\" is thread local\n * (every OS thread has a separate current stream, as one might expect),\n * the stream pool is global across all threads; stream 0 is always stream 0\n * no matter which thread you use it on.  Multiple threads can synchronize\n * on the same stream.  Although the CUDA documentation is not very clear\n * on the matter, streams are thread safe; e.g., it is safe to enqueue\n * a kernel on the same stream from two different threads.\n */\n\nnamespace c10::cuda {\n\nstatic constexpr int max_compile_time_stream_priorities = 4;\n\n// Value object representing a CUDA stream.  This is just a wrapper\n// around c10::Stream, but it comes with a little extra CUDA-specific\n// functionality (conversion to cudaStream_t), and a guarantee that\n// the wrapped c10::Stream really is a CUDA stream.\nclass C10_CUDA_API CUDAStream {\n public:\n  enum Unchecked { UNCHECKED };\n\n  /// Construct a CUDAStream from a Stream.  This construction is checked,\n  /// and will raise an error if the Stream is not, in fact, a CUDA stream.\n  explicit CUDAStream(Stream stream) : stream_(stream) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(stream_.device_type() == DeviceType::CUDA);\n  }\n\n  /// Construct a CUDAStream from a Stream with no error checking.\n  /// This constructor uses the \"named\" constructor idiom, and can\n  /// be invoked as: CUDAStream(CUDAStream::UNCHECKED, stream)\n  explicit CUDAStream(Unchecked /*unused*/, Stream stream) : stream_(stream) {}\n\n  bool operator==(const CUDAStream& other) const noexcept {\n    return unwrap() == other.unwrap();\n  }\n\n  bool operator!=(const CUDAStream& other) const noexcept {\n    return unwrap() != other.unwrap();\n  }\n\n  /// Implicit conversion to cudaStream_t.\n  operator cudaStream_t() const {\n    return stream();\n  }\n\n  /// Implicit conversion to Stream (a.k.a., forget that the stream is a\n  /// CUDA stream).\n  operator Stream() const {\n    return unwrap();\n  }\n\n  /// Used to avoid baking in device type explicitly to Python-side API.\n  DeviceType device_type() const {\n    return DeviceType::CUDA;\n  }\n\n  /// Get the CUDA device index that this stream is associated with.","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":100,"totalLines":273,"truncated":true}},"title":"opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/torch/include/c10/cuda/CUDAStream.h","time":{"start":1784470550916,"end":1784470550923}},"id":"prt_f7abbb980001RYUoAFNkIxTH63","sessionID":"ses_085491119ffeTM7pHc2vYiiVB9","messageID":"msg_f7abba3e3001Zj7COTR3tPdjnt"}}

HIPStreams\n * are backed by cuStreams, but they use several pools to minimize the costs\n * associated with creating, retaining, and destroying cuStreams.\n *\n * There are three pools per device, and a device's pools are lazily created.\n *\n * The first pool contains only the default stream. When the default stream\n * is requested it's returned.\n *\n * The second pool is the \"low priority\" or \"default priority\" streams. In\n * HIP builds there is no distinction between streams in this pool and streams\n * in the third pool (below). There are 32 of these streams per device, and\n * when a stream is requested one of these streams is returned round-robin.\n * That is, the first stream requested is at index 0, the second at index 1...\n * to index 31, then index 0 again.\n *\n * This means that if 33 low priority streams are requested, the first and\n * last streams requested are actually the same stream (under the covers)\n * and kernels enqueued on them cannot run concurrently.\n *\n * The third pool is the \"high priority\" streams. The third pool acts like\n * the second pool except the streams are created with a higher priority.\n *\n * These pools suggest that stream users should prefer many short-lived streams,\n * as the cost of acquiring and releasing streams is effectively zero. If\n * many longer-lived streams are required in performance critical scenarios\n * then the functionality here may need to be extended to allow, for example,\n * \"reserving\" a subset of the pool so that other streams do not accidentally\n * overlap the performance critical streams.\n *\n * Note: although the notion of \"current stream for device\" is thread local\n * (every OS thread has a separate current stream, as one might expect),\n * the stream pool is global across all threads; stream 0 is always stream 0\n * no matter which thread you use it on.  Multiple threads can synchronize\n * on the same stream. Let me also do one more verification: check that the build artifacts are actually needed by the grader, OR if they should be cleaned. The only manual changes needed are in the build configuration (`setup.py` and the JIT fallback in `pointnet2_utils.py`) to use ROCm-compatible flags and include paths.\n\nLet me also check if there are any remaining issues.
